title: waltheria matogrossensis
intro: Waltheria matogrossensis, also known as the Waltheria plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Waltheria matogrossensis include the potential treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiparasitic agent to combat intestinal worms. Herbal preparations of Waltheria matogrossensis are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for use in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
